title: Make assign_next operation_id idempotent
goal: Implement a bounded issue #13 idempotency slice so explicit operation_id deduplicates assign_next retries that would otherwise pick a different next task after the first assignment succeeds.
requiredInputs: Existing swarm mutation state AssignTask response support
Current assign_next target_session=None selects selected_task_id before assign_task mutation
permissions: Modify protocol, swarm tool, assign_next server handler, and focused tests
Run cargo fmt/check/test and selfdev build
completionConditions: CommAssignNext accepts an optional request_nonce propagated from swarm operation_id for explicit retry idempotency.
When assign_next is called without target_session and with request_nonce, duplicate calls replay the original AssignTask response instead of selecting the next unassigned task.
assign_next behavior without operation_id remains unchanged so intentional repeated assign_next calls can still assign additional tasks.
Focused protocol/server/tool tests, cargo fmt/check, git diff --check, selfdev build/reload, governance, commit, and push pass.
failureTaxonomy: Changing default assign_next behavior without operation_id
Persisting outer assign_next responses before assignment side effects finish
Over-expanding into full task-control idempotency or SwarmRun persistence
notes: Keep the slice to CommAssignNext explicit request_nonce support and target_session=None path. Do not make default assign_next globally idempotent.
